ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees
Off Calendar May-24-2021 Continued To Jun-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica McGowan-Vanderbeck, via ZOOM, in Propria Persona, Administrator; Diana Kruze, via Zoom, Administrator's cousin.

The Petition in this matter was not set for hearing on this morning's calendar as it had been pre-granted. Ms. McGowan-Vanderbeck and Ms. Kruz request to appear before the court to address the Examiner's notes regarding the pre-granted order which had been revised to require distribution to trustee Walter McGowan. The court advises that Walter Mcgowan listed himself as the sole trustee in a declaration filed with the court, signed under penalty of perjury. Ms. McGowan-Vanderbeck reports that she believes that her and her uncle, Walter McGowan, are co-trustees as listed in a trust from 2019. As stated in the Examiner's notes, this court does not order distribution directly from a probate estate to a trust beneficiary when the will orders distribution to the trustee.

After discussion, the Accounting petition is continued to June 9, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204 for the submission of supporting documents.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

***Upon further consideration, the court will be requesting that the petitioner file a copy of the Trust dated 2019 prior to the June 9th hearing. The court will advise petitioner of the preceding.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-19-2021 Continued To May-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Feb-10-2021 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica McGowan Vanderbeck appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Diana Kruze (Cousin of Jessica) appeared by zoom to provide emotional support.

The Court questions what the basis is for filing this petition, Ms. Vanderbeck's level of knowledge about probate, and whether Ms. Vanderbeck knows what she can and cannot do. Ms. Vanderbeck stated that she was left with a great deal of assets in her Father's trust and estate, that she only realized later on that this one asset was left out of the trust, that she has learned a lot about probate and that she has had conversations with the Clerks. Ms. Vanderbeck also stated that she knows that she cannot take ownership of the account and that she must ask the Court for permission to use any of the funds. The Court informed Ms. Vanderbeck that she is required to file an inventory and appraisal, that she is expected to administer the estate properly, and that she will need to file a petition for final distribution within an expected amount of time. The Court also informed Ms. Vanderbeck that the funds are to be deposited in a proper account in the name of the estate and that she cannot co-mingle her own funds with the estate funds. Ms. Vanderbeck stated that she believes there is no bond pursuant to the terms of the will and that all of the other parties have given her permission. The will dated February 2, 1994 is admitted to probate. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition. Bond is not required. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for February 10, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and is to be filed by February 3, 2021. Status on close of estate is set for January 12, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. Vanderbeck that either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration needs to be filed one week prior.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-26-2020 Continued To Sep-23-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
